title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Guidelines for the Use of Fluorides Publication date: 2 November 2009 The Guidelines for the Use of Fluorides provide an evidence-based summary of current New Zealand and overseas evidence to inform best practice in the use of fluoride. Publication Oral health

## Guidelines for the Use of Fluorides
The Guidelines for the Use of Fluorides provide an evidence-based summary of current New Zealand and overseas evidence to inform best practice in the use of fluoride.
- Management of Early Breast Cancer: Evidence-based Best Practice Guideline Publication date: 2 September 2009 The purpose of this guideline is to provide an evidence-based summary of best practice in the management of early breast cancer in order to promote best clinical practice in relation to the care and management of women with early breast cancer. Publication Cancer

## Management of Early Breast Cancer: Evidence-based Best Practice Guideline
The purpose of this guideline is to provide an evidence-based summary of best practice in the management of early breast cancer in order to promote best clinical practice in relation to the care and management of women with early breast cancer.
- Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT) in New Zealand: What you and your family and whānau need to know Publication date: 2 August 2009 Every person for whom electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) is a treatment choice has the right to up-to-date information about ECT in New Zealand. This consumer information pack provides information and answers to the many questions someone considering ECT as a treatment choice, is likely to have about ECT. Publication Mental health

## Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT) in New Zealand: What you and your family and whānau need to know
Every person for whom electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) is a treatment choice has the right to up-to-date information about ECT in New Zealand. This consumer information pack provides information and answers to the many questions someone considering ECT as a treatment choice, is likely to have about ECT.
- Roles and Responsibilities for Supporting Disabled Children and Young People under the Children, Young Persons, and Their Families Act 1989 Publication date: 1 March 2009 The Ministry of Social Development and the Ministry of Health have a memorandum of understanding between them that relates to disabled children and young people who meet the eligibility criteria for health and disability services and who are subject to Part Two of the Children, Young Persons, and Their Families Act 1989. Publication Disability Child health

## Roles and Responsibilities for Supporting Disabled Children and Young People under the Children, Young Persons, and Their Families Act 1989
The Ministry of Social Development and the Ministry of Health have a memorandum of understanding between them that relates to disabled children and young people who meet the eligibility criteria for health and disability services and who are subject to Part Two of the Children, Young Persons, and Their Families Act 1989.
- Guidelines for the Management of Norovirus Outbreaks in Hospitals and Elderly Care Institutions Publication date: 2 January 2009 These 2009 guidelines were developed to standardise the approach of public health services, managers and health care workers of hospitals and elderly care facilities in New Zealand to both the investigation and control of institutional norovirus outbreaks Publication Notifiable diseases

## Guidelines for the Management of Norovirus Outbreaks in Hospitals and Elderly Care Institutions
These 2009 guidelines were developed to standardise the approach of public health services, managers and health care workers of hospitals and elderly care facilities in New Zealand to both the investigation and control of institutional norovirus outbreaks
- National Health Emergency Plan: Guidance on community-based assessment centres and other support services Publication date: 2 December 2008 Community-based assessment centres (CBACs) are one of the elements planned for responding to a national health emergency, especially where there are large numbers of people involved. This document aims to provide guidance to DHBs and primary health organisations on the role and function of CBACs, home-based services and teletriage in a health emergency. Publication Emergency management

## National Health Emergency Plan: Guidance on community-based assessment centres and other support services
Community-based assessment centres (CBACs) are one of the elements planned for responding to a national health emergency, especially where there are large numbers of people involved. This document aims to provide guidance to DHBs and primary health organisations on the role and function of CBACs, home-based services and teletriage in a health emergency.
- Emergency management
- Treaty of Waitangi Deed of Settlement - Contemporary Aspects of the Napier Hospital and Health Services Claim (Wai 692) Publication date: 3 November 2008 A Deed of Settlement between the Crown and the claimants, for the Contemporary Aspects of the Wai 692 - Napier Hospital and Health Services Claim, was signed by the Hon Mita Ririnui, Associate Minister of Health and the claimants in Napier on Friday 3 October 2008. Publication Health system

## Treaty of Waitangi Deed of Settlement - Contemporary Aspects of the Napier Hospital and Health Services Claim (Wai 692)
A Deed of Settlement between the Crown and the claimants, for the Contemporary Aspects of the Wai 692 - Napier Hospital and Health Services Claim, was signed by the Hon Mita Ririnui, Associate Minister of Health and the claimants in Napier on Friday 3 October 2008.
- Guidelines for Public Health Services on Cluster Control for Pandemic Influenza Publication date: 2 July 2008 These guidelines describe the public health interventions that may be used by Public Health Units in the cluster control phase of pandemic influenza. Publication Emergency management Influenza Pandemics Public health

## Guidelines for Public Health Services on Cluster Control for Pandemic Influenza
These guidelines describe the public health interventions that may be used by Public Health Units in the cluster control phase of pandemic influenza.
- Emergency management
- The Health Equity Assessment Tool: A User's Guide Publication date: 1 July 2008 The Health Equity Assessment Tool: A User's Guide, has been developed by the University of Otago, Wellington, to help facilitate the use of the Health Equity Assessment Tool (HEAT). Publication Health system Inequalities

## The Health Equity Assessment Tool: A User's Guide
The Health Equity Assessment Tool: A User's Guide, has been developed by the University of Otago, Wellington, to help facilitate the use of the Health Equity Assessment Tool (HEAT).
- Pacific Cultural Competencies: A Literature Review Publication date: 2 February 2008 An overview of the literature on Pacific cultural competence in health care including definitions of Pacific cultural competence, rationale for and benefits of cultural competence, competence measures and mechanisms, role of Pacific cultural competence in service quality and recommendations for the New Zealand health and disability sector. Publication Pacific health

## Pacific Cultural Competencies: A Literature Review
An overview of the literature on Pacific cultural competence in health care including definitions of Pacific cultural competence, rationale for and benefits of cultural competence, competence measures and mechanisms, role of Pacific cultural competence in service quality and recommendations for the New Zealand health and disability sector.
- Guidelines for the Control of Multidrug-resistant Organisms in New Zealand Publication date: 2 December 2007 The Guidelines for the Control of Multidrug-resistant Organisms in New Zealand are a resource for DHBs to develop own their local guidelines to control Multidrug-resistant organisms (MDROs). Publication Antimicrobial resistance

## Guidelines for the Control of Multidrug-resistant Organisms in New Zealand
The Guidelines for the Control of Multidrug-resistant Organisms in New Zealand are a resource for DHBs to develop own their local guidelines to control Multidrug-resistant organisms (MDROs).
- Antimicrobial resistance
- Implementing and Monitoring the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes in NZ: The Code in NZ Publication date: 6 August 2007 Breastfeeding is key to providing the best start for New Zealand infants and it is important for both mother and baby’s health. Following a review of the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes in New Zealand, the Ministry has prepared a single, standard reference document which now includes the Code of Practice for Health Workers in New Zealand and the New Zealand Infant Formula Marketers’ Association Code of Practice for the Marketing of Infant Formula. Publication Breast-milk substitutes Maternity

## Implementing and Monitoring the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes in NZ: The Code in NZ
Breastfeeding is key to providing the best start for New Zealand infants and it is important for both mother and baby’s health. Following a review of the International Code of Marketing of Breast-milk Substitutes in New Zealand, the Ministry has prepared a single, standard reference document which now includes the Code of Practice for Health Workers in New Zealand and the New Zealand Infant Formula Marketers’ Association Code of Practice for the Marketing of Infant Formula.
- Breast-milk substitutes
- Guidelines for the Use of Human Tissue for Future Unspecified Research Purposes Publication date: 14 May 2007 Guidelines allowing New Zealand individuals to give consent to their tissue being used for future unspecified research, subject to having been provided with sufficient information and options for consent. Publication Human tissue

## Guidelines for the Use of Human Tissue for Future Unspecified Research Purposes
Guidelines allowing New Zealand individuals to give consent to their tissue being used for future unspecified research, subject to having been provided with sufficient information and options for consent.
- The National Childhood Immunisation Coverage Survey 2005 Publication date: 2 April 2007 The National Childhood Immunisation Survey 2005 involved interviewing the caregivers of 1563 children aged two to three years old throughout the country from January to March 2005. The study looked at immunisation coverage of the primary series of vaccinations up to the age of two years. Caregiver responses were verified from written records. Publication Babies and toddlers (0–2 years) Immunisation Statistics Surveys

## The National Childhood Immunisation Coverage Survey 2005
The National Childhood Immunisation Survey 2005 involved interviewing the caregivers of 1563 children aged two to three years old throughout the country from January to March 2005. The study looked at immunisation coverage of the primary series of vaccinations up to the age of two years. Caregiver responses were verified from written records.
- Babies and toddlers (0–2 years)
- Whānau Ora Health Impact Assessment 2007 Publication date: 2 April 2007 The Whānau Ora Health Impact Assessment tool is a formal approach used to predict the potential health effects of a policy on Māori and their whānau. It pays particular attention to Māori involvement in the policy development process and articulates the role of the wider health determinants in influencing health and well-being outcomes. Publication Public health Māori health

## Whānau Ora Health Impact Assessment 2007
The Whānau Ora Health Impact Assessment tool is a formal approach used to predict the potential health effects of a policy on Māori and their whānau. It pays particular attention to Māori involvement in the policy development process and articulates the role of the wider health determinants in influencing health and well-being outcomes.
